protocol_name: Head and Neck Cancer – Cisplatin (100) RT
indication: Radical treatment of squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 120 minutes). Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.

pre_medications.frequency: three times a day when required

pre_medications.drug_name: Sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate
Sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate

take_home_medicines.duration: 3 days starting evening of cisplatin day

take_home_medicines.frequency: three times a day when required

other.action: Dose modification necessary. Consider stopping for NCI-CTC grade 3 or more neurotoxicity or ototoxicity.

treatment_delay_criteria: Delay treatment if neutrophils < 1.5 x10^9/L or platelets < 100 x10^9/L
Delay up to 2 weeks for NCI-CTC grade 3+ toxicities until resolved to grade 1 or less
monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
Patient advised to drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after cisplatin administration
special_warnings: Cisplatin is an exfoliant - extravasation risk
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
Cisplatin: mandatory IV hydration required — pre-hydration and post-hydration per local cisplatin hydration protocol. Monitor urine output, U&Es, and Mg²⁺ before and after each cycle.

drugs.special_instructions: Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 120 minutes). Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.
drugs.notes: Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 120 minutes). Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.

protocol_name: Head and Neck Cancer – Cisplatin (40) RT
indication: Radical treatment of locally advanced head and neck cancer when surgery is inappropriate

take_home_medicines.duration: 2 days starting day two of cycle

take_home_medicines.frequency: three times a day for 2 days then when required

take_home_medicines.duration: 2 days starting evening of day one

Counts not recovered at 7 days - delay further 7 days, counts satisfactory at 14 days

other.action: Dose modification necessary. Consider stopping for NCI-CTC grade 3 or more neurotoxicity or ototoxicity.
Delay cycle up to 2 weeks until resolves to grade 1 or less. Delay beyond 2 weeks requires discontinuation.

Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed

indication: Neoadjuvant or advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: 20mg/m² daily on days 1-4 (total 80mg/m² per cycle). Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 60 minutes). Pre-hydration with 500ml NaCl 0.9% with 8mmol MgSO4 over 30 minutes before each cisplatin dose. Dose banded per national bands (1mg/ml).
Infusion over 60 minutes on day 2 only. Doses >200mg dilute in 500ml NaCl 0.9% (max concentration 0.74mg/ml). Dose banded per national bands (20mg/ml). Docetaxel is an exfoliant - extravasation risk.
700mg/m²/day continuous infusion over 22 hours each day (total 2800mg/m² per cycle). Dose banded per national bands (50mg/ml). Fluorouracil is an inflammitant - extravasation risk.

pre_medications.timing: Three times a day when required
pre_medications.frequency: three times a day when required

pre_medications.notes: Pre-cisplatin hydration over 30 minutes before each cisplatin dose

take_home_medicines.duration: 2 days then 2mg once daily for 2 days starting day 5

other.action: Delay until resolves to grade 1; consider dose reduction

special_warnings: InP (inpatient) regimen - patient requires inpatient admission for treatment
DPD deficiency: patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity with fluorouracil - test all patients before cycle 1
This regimen has been locally adapted from the TPF references (TAX 323, TAX 324) - locally modified doses
Cisplatin given as 20mg/m²/day x4 days (total 80mg/m²); Fluorouracil given as 700mg/m²/day x4 days over 22 hours (total 2800mg/m²); Docetaxel given on day 2 only at 40mg/m²
Pre-cisplatin hydration with 500ml NaCl 0.9% + 8mmol MgSO4 over 30 minutes before each of the 4 daily cisplatin doses

Mouthwashes as per local or national guidelines
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
Blood transfusion if symptomatic anaemia or Hb < 8g/dL

drugs.special_instructions: 20mg/m² daily on days 1-4 (total 80mg/m² per cycle). Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 60 minutes). Pre-hydration with 500ml NaCl 0.9% with 8mmol MgSO4 over 30 minutes before each cisplatin dose. Dose banded per national bands (1mg/ml).

indication: Advanced head and neck cancer; WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Methotrexate is an inflammitant - extravasation risk.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1
15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1

take_home_medicines.frequency: three times a day when required

take_home_medicines.duration: 2 days starting 24 hours after methotrexate administration
take_home_medicines.notes: Folinic acid rescue - MANDATORY. 15mg QDS x2 days starting 24 hours post-methotrexate.

rules.action: Delay 7 days; resume at original dose or 80% depending on clinical circumstances

rules.condition: Second episode of neutropenia/thrombocytopenia or recovery takes > 7 days

treatment_delay_criteria: Delay 7 days if neutrophils < 1.0 x10^9/L or platelets < 100 x10^9/L
monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs prior to each cycle
Blood transfusion if symptomatic anaemia or Hb < 9.5g/dL
special_warnings: Folinic acid rescue MANDATORY: 15mg orally four times daily for 2 days starting 24 hours after methotrexate administration
Presence of third fluid compartment (e.g. ascites or renal failure) may delay methotrexate clearance and increase toxicity
Methotrexate is an inflammitant - extravasation risk
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed

drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Methotrexate is an inflammitant - extravasation risk.

protocol_name: Head and Neck Cancer – Nivolumab (240mg-14 days)
indication: Treatment of recurrent or metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck after platinum-based chemotherapy. Criteria: recurrent/metastatic HNSCC not amenable to local curative therapy; disease progressed or recurred during or within 6 months of last platinum-based chemotherapy; PD-L1 TPS testing performed; no symptomatic active brain/leptomeningeal metastases; no prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1/PD-L2/CD137/CTLA-4 therapy; monotherapy; WHO PS 0-1

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose 240mg. Infusion over 30 minutes via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter (polyethylene-lined giving sets used for paclitaxel with 0.22 micron filter are appropriate). Dose is NOT reduced for toxicity - doses are delayed only. Nivolumab is neutral - minimal extravasation risk.

pre_medications.notes: For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only

rules.action: No dose reduction required. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended.

hepatic.action: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper over 1 month; restart when LFTs grade 1 after taper

renal.condition: Pre-existing mild or moderate renal impairment
Immune-related nephritis: grade 2 or 3 creatinine elevation
renal.action: Withhold and initiate corticosteroids; taper; restart when creatinine grade 1 or below

immune_related_adverse_events.action: Withhold until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, corticosteroid management complete
Permanently discontinue
Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete
Permanently discontinue
Withhold until laboratory values return to baseline and corticosteroid management complete
Permanently discontinue
Withhold until creatinine returns to baseline and corticosteroid management complete
Permanently discontinue

immune_related_adverse_events.grade: Symptomatic (hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, hypophysitis, adrenal insufficiency, diabetes)
immune_related_adverse_events.action: Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete. Continue nivolumab with hormone replacement therapy if asymptomatic.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete
Permanently discontinue

other.action: Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer

treatment_delay_criteria: Doses are DELAYED not reduced for immunotherapy toxicity
Withhold for grade 2 pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis, renal impairment, and grade 3 other toxicities
Do not resume if patient still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids
Permanently discontinue if toxicity does not improve to grade 1 within 12 weeks of last dose
Funding approval precludes treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer
monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment then every 6 weeks or when clinically indicated
52 cycles set in Aria
Treatment stopped at 2 years or disease progression or unacceptable toxicity, whichever first
special_warnings: IMMUNOTHERAPY: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for nivolumab. Doses are delayed only.
Nivolumab 240mg is a FLAT DOSE - not weight or BSA based
Patient MUST be given a Nivolumab Patient Alert Card
Avoid systemic corticosteroids BEFORE starting nivolumab (may interfere with efficacy); may use after starting for immune-related AEs
Autoimmune toxicities can be life-threatening; seek specialist guidance for management
Atypical responses (initial increase in tumour size then shrinkage) can occur; continue treatment in clinically stable patients with initial progression until confirmed
PD-L1 TPS testing required; every effort should be made to obtain TPS
Isolated hypothyroidism can be managed with replacement therapy without treatment interruption
Adrenal crisis (severe dehydration, hypotension, shock): immediate IV corticosteroids with mineralocorticoid activity
For grade 2-3 immune-related AEs persisting despite treatment or unable to reduce corticosteroids to <= 10mg prednisolone equivalent per day: permanently discontinue
Loperamide 4mg orally initially then 2mg after each loose stool when required (maximum 16mg/24 hours)
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed

indication: First-line treatment of metastatic or locally advanced/unresectable recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck in adult patients. Criteria: documented histological diagnosis of HNSCC; metastatic or locally advanced/unresectable recurrent HNSCC not amenable to curative local therapy; PD-L1 CPS >= 1 (not funded if CPS < 1); ECOG PS 0 or 1; no prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1/PD-L2/CD137/CTLA-4 therapy (unless prior pembrolizumab monotherapy via Interim COVID19 funding); no symptomatic active brain/leptomeningeal metastases; monotherapy only

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose 200mg. Infusion over 30 minutes with 0.2 micron low protein binding filter. Dose is NOT reduced for toxicity - doses are delayed only. Pembrolizumab is neutral - minimal extravasation risk.

pre_medications.notes: For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only. Maximum dose 4g/24h; 4 hours between doses.

hepatic.action: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper over 1 month; restart when LFTs grade 1 after taper

hepatic.condition: Immune-related hepatitis: AST/ALT >= 5xULN or bilirubin > 3xULN, or AST/ALT rise >= 50% from baseline lasting >= 1 week in liver metastasis patients (Grade 3-4)

renal.action: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper; restart when creatinine grade 1 or below

Permanently discontinue

Permanently discontinue

Ophthalmologist evaluation; topical corticosteroids eye drops and iridocyclitics; discontinue if persists despite topical immunosuppression
Discontinue pembrolizumab; systemic corticosteroids; taper over >= 4 weeks when improved to grade 1
Symptomatic treatment (topical corticosteroids/urea creams with oral antipruritics); continue pembrolizumab
Withhold; dermatology referral; systemic prednisolone 1mg/kg daily; taper when improved to grade 1
Permanently discontinue

special_warnings: IMMUNOTHERAPY: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab. Doses are delayed only.
Pembrolizumab 200mg is a FLAT DOSE - not weight or BSA based

Avoid systemic corticosteroids BEFORE starting pembrolizumab (may interfere with efficacy); may use after starting for immune-related AEs

Serious skin reactions including SJS and TEN possible

Permanently discontinue for grade 3-4 pneumonitis, nephritis, or infusion-related reactions, or grade 4 adverse reactions

indication: First-line treatment of metastatic or locally advanced/unresectable recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck in adult patients. Criteria: documented histological diagnosis of HNSCC; metastatic or locally advanced/unresectable recurrent HNSCC not amenable to curative local therapy; PD-L1 CPS >= 1 (not funded if CPS < 1); ECOG PS 0 or 1; no prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1/PD-L2/CD137/CTLA-4 therapy (unless prior pembrolizumab monotherapy via Interim COVID19 funding); no symptomatic active brain/leptomeningeal metastases; monotherapy only; equivalent to 35 x 3-weekly cycles of 200mg

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose 400mg every 6 weeks. Infusion over 30 minutes with 0.2 micron low protein binding filter. Dose is NOT reduced for toxicity - doses are delayed only. Pembrolizumab is neutral - minimal extravasation risk. Equivalent to 35 x 3-weekly cycles of 200mg (18 x 6-weekly cycles = 2 years).

pre_medications.notes: For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only
For treatment of infusion-related reactions only. Maximum dose 4g/24h; 4 hours between doses.

hepatic.action: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper over 1 month; restart when LFTs grade 1 after taper

hepatic.condition: Immune-related hepatitis: AST/ALT >= 5xULN or bilirubin > 3xULN, or AST/ALT rise >= 50% from baseline lasting >= 1 week in liver metastasis patients (Grade 3-4)

renal.action: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper; restart when creatinine grade 1 or below

immune_related_adverse_events.action: Continue with close monitoring; follow on serial imaging
Withhold; corticosteroids 1-2mg/kg/day prednisone; restart if improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and steroids reduced to prednisolone 10mg oral daily equivalent
Permanently discontinue
Continue; symptomatic treatment and close monitoring
Withhold and administer corticosteroids; taper; restart when colitis grade 1
Permanently discontinue
Withhold and corticosteroids; restart when LFTs grade 1
Permanently discontinue
Withhold and corticosteroids; restart when grade 1
Permanently discontinue
Manage with hormone replacement; corticosteroids for acute inflammation. Isolated hypothyroidism: replacement therapy without interruption.
Ophthalmologist evaluation; topical corticosteroids eye drops and iridocyclitics; discontinue if persists despite topical immunosuppression
Discontinue pembrolizumab; systemic corticosteroids; taper over >= 4 weeks when improved to grade 1
Symptomatic treatment (topical corticosteroids/urea creams with oral antipruritics); continue pembrolizumab
Withhold; dermatology referral; systemic prednisolone 1mg/kg daily; taper when improved to grade 1
Permanently discontinue

other.action: Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer
